---
title: "Workshops & Outreach"
listing:
  id: listing-projects
  contents: "projects/outreach/*.qmd"
  type: default
  sort: "date desc"
  categories: true
---

My outreach work focuses on helping students, educators, professionals, public agencies, and community partners use geospatial tools more effectively. These activities often connect GIS, cartography, UAVs, remote sensing, and professional development with real-world needs beyond a traditional classroom setting.

Workshops and outreach events allow me to translate technical geospatial topics into practical learning experiences for different audiences, including undergraduate students, K--12 educators, working professionals, community organizations, and public-sector partners.

## Outreach Areas

- **FAA Part 107 and UAV Education:** Workshops and learning materials that help participants understand airspace, regulations, weather, sectional charts, airport operations, and safe UAV operations.
- **GIS Professional Development:** Training opportunities focused on practical GIS workflows, geospatial problem solving, map communication, and applied spatial analysis.
- **K--12 Educator Support:** Professional development activities that help teachers connect geospatial tools, UAVs, maps, and spatial thinking to classroom learning.
- **Community and Public Outreach:** Events and projects that make geospatial technology more accessible to community partners and public audiences.
- **Student Professional Development:** Activities that help students prepare for conferences, internships, research opportunities, portfolios, and careers in GIS and geospatial technology.

## Approach

I try to design workshops around what participants actually need to do after the event. Rather than focusing only on software demonstrations, I emphasize practical workflows, decision points, common mistakes, and the reasoning behind technical steps.

This approach is especially important in geospatial outreach because participants often need to apply what they learn in complex, real-world settings. A useful workshop should help people understand not only which buttons to press, but also why the workflow matters, what limitations to watch for, and how to communicate results clearly.

## Selected Outreach Examples

The examples below highlight public-facing workshops, educator resources, professional-development programs, StoryMaps, data hubs, and geospatial learning materials developed for K–12 educators, students, and professional audiences.

::: {#listing-projects}
:::
